title: NH authorities seeking ‘violent,’ ‘armed and dangerous’ fugitive
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lisa Marie Lundstrom, 47, is wanted for absconding from probation in New Hampshire and Massachusetts.
Lundstrom has an extensive criminal history, including charges of armed robbery, burglary, and drug offenses.
Authorities describe Lundstrom as armed and dangerous, urging the public to contact 911 if seen.

title: Local obituary: Kai Prescott and Ella Brayton MacAusland, Wellesley siblings
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Wellesley siblings Kai, 7, and Ella MacAusland, 6, passed away on April 24, 2026.
Funeral service at St. Andrew’s Episcopal Church, Wellesley on May 16 at 2 p.m.
Donations in memory of Kai and Ella can be made to The Schofield PTO, Wellesley.

title: Fidelity reorganizes its workplace with new hires — and a few cuts
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fidelity Investments plans to hire thousands while eliminating about 800 jobs as part of its reorganization strategy.
The company is restructuring tech and product teams with 2,000 open positions, including 400 in tech roles.
Fidelity manages $7 trillion in assets and plans to transition its workforce to a new campus at Commonwealth Pier.

title: Here are the Celtics’ pick numbers in the 2026 NBA Draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics have the 27th overall pick in the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Boston holds the 40th overall pick in the second round via Milwaukee and Orlando.
Previous Celtics first-round picks Payton Pritchard and Hugo Gonzalez were selected 26th and 28th, respectively.

title: Latest Red Line shutdown kicks in this week
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The MBTA Red Line service will be suspended from Broadway to Ashmont starting Thursday through Monday, May 18.
Free shuttle buses and accessible vans will operate between Broadway and Ashmont during the suspension.
The suspension supports signal modernization at Columbia Junction, following damage from a 2019 derailment.

title: Accused serial rapist Alvin Campbell Jr. — the AG’s brother — stands trial this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., brother of Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell, stands trial for charges including rape, kidnapping, and indecent assault.
Prosecutors allege Campbell posed as a rideshare driver, targeting intoxicated women in Boston, with nine alleged victims from 2017-2019.
Campbell's trial will combine multiple dockets, as the judge granted the request citing a pattern of targeting intoxicated female victims.

title: The surprisingly small world behind Cambridge and Somerville’s cafe scene
author: Josh Breeden
bullets: Vinal Bakery founder Sarah Murphy honed her skills at Bagelsaurus Cambridge, helped by owner Mary Ting Hyatt's mentorship.
Forge Baking Co. owners operate four cafes in Cambridge-Somerville, building a network with interconnected menus and community ties.
New Leaf Espresso in Somerville, run by 1369 Coffee House alumni, reflects the cooperative spirit and innovation of the local cafe scene.

title: Person killed in Mattapan shooting, 2 others shot in Dorchester overnight Saturday
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was fatally shot outside 28 Woolson St. in Mattapan on Saturday night.
Two people were injured in a shooting on Geneva Ave. in Dorchester early Sunday morning.
No arrests have been made; police are investigating both shootings in Boston this weekend.

title: Kevin Garnett wasn’t a fan of Jayson Tatum missing Game 7 vs. Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Garnett expressed shock over Jayson Tatum's absence in Game 7 against the Sixers.
Tatum was ruled out due to left knee stiffness, despite initial expectations he would play.
Paul Pierce defended the decision, citing long-term health risks for playing Tatum.

title: 2 people wounded, 1 seriously, after shooting in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A shooting on Kingsdale Street in Dorchester left a woman with life-threatening injuries and a man with non-life-threatening injuries.
Boston police responded to 911 calls about gunshots at 69 Kingsdale St. around 6:40 p.m. Sunday night.
No arrests have been made; police believe the shooting was targeted and assure the public there's no broader threat.
